assessments.status_rationale: Today’s items do not mention any House or Senate passage of a bill restricting/prohibiting sports-related prediction markets/event contracts. No legislative action evidence is provided.
No news bullet references a CFTC order, enforcement filing, Commission vote notice, or other formal public action naming a platform (e.g., Kalshi/Polymarket) about sports event contracts.

Multiple stories discuss the LaGuardia crash/investigation, but the bullets do not clearly indicate that the NTSB has published a preliminary report. Without explicit confirmation, the prediction remains unresolved.

Today’s coverage includes airport delay/TSA-line stories and LaGuardia crash reporting, but it does not cite any FAA SAFO/Safety Alert/Order (or equivalent) specifically addressing ground-vehicle runway/surface collision prevention tied to the LaGuardia incident.

No bullet indicates Harvard entered a publicly posted federal resolution agreement/settlement tied to a 2026-opened civil-rights investigation. No relevant federal civil-rights settlement news is present.
No items mention DOI/BOEM cancelling/suspending/terminating an offshore wind project/lease area, nor any disclosed compensation/settlement payments totaling $500M related to such action.
There is no Apple-related announcement/newsroom post/developer documentation/iOS release note cited today regarding paid advertising placements in Apple Maps.
No bullet discusses private-credit interval or tender-offer funds (Apollo/Blackstone/KKR/Ares/Blue Owl) or reports of consecutive redemption windows with 50% or less fulfillment.

While there is airline-related news (e.g., Delta services for Congress) and airport delay/TSA-line coverage, there is no evidence from earnings transcripts or SEC filings that at least two major airlines explicitly attributed measurable disruption to ATC staffing/operational constraints.
